section.heading: Assistive technology, assistive technology devices, and assistive technology services

subsection.heading: Authority.—
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary of Defense may provide assistive technology, assistive technology devices, and assistive technology services to the following:

paragraph.content: Department of Defense employees with disabilities.
Organizations within the Department that have requirements to make programs or facilities accessible to, and usable by, persons with disabilities.

Any other department or agency of the Federal Government, upon the request of the head of that department or agency, for its employees with disabilities or for satisfying a requirement to make its programs or facilities accessible to, and usable by, persons with disabilities.

subsection.heading: Definitions.—
subsection.content: In this section, the terms “assistive technology”, “assistive technology device”, “assistive technology service”, and “disability” have the meanings given those terms in section 3 of the Assistive Technology Act of 1998 (29 U.S.C. 3002).

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 1582, acts Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 1041, 70A Stat. 118; Sept. 2, 1958, Pub. L. 85–861, § 1(35), 72 Stat. 1456; Sept. 23, 1959, Pub. L. 86–377, § 3, 73 Stat. 701, directed Secretary of Defense to report annually to Congress on civilian research and development personnel employed by Department of Defense under former section 1581 of this title, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 97–295, § 1(19)(A), Oct. 12, 1982, 96 Stat. 1290.
